title: UK alcohol deaths fall for first time since Covid pandemic
snippet: Experts say the “modest reduction” is “not cause for complacency”, with calls to redouble efforts to slash deaths further.

title: Daily pill to help keep weight off after stopping obesity jabs
snippet: The tablet - orforglipron - is available in the US and could soon launch in the UK.

title: Elon Musk said control of OpenAI should go to his children, Sam Altman tells jury
snippet: Sam Altman said Elon Musk tried many times for total control of OpenAI, which he's now suing.

title: 'We're living in a shed because of river pollution'
snippet: Jane and Tony Coyle spent seven years waiting for planning permission due to River Lugg pollution.

title: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble of Kyiv flats after massive Russian strikes
snippet: Two children are among at least 16 killed in Ukraine's capital in massive Russian drone and missile attacks, officials say.

title: Trump brought top CEOs to Beijing but few big deals emerge
snippet: There was plenty of choreographed ceremonies but there was no sweeping trade breakthrough as Trump met Xi in Beijing.

title: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows
snippet: As El Niño develops this year, scientists are increasingly confident it could be one of the strongest on record with global consequences, as Simon King explains.

title: More than 70 million warnings sent to people seeking child abuse material
snippet: The messages highlight the illegality of what people are searching for online and direct them towards help.

title: Is this actually what Anne Boleyn looked like?
snippet: An algorithm has suggested images of what were thought to be Anne Boleyn might not be.

title: Kerr to leave Chelsea at end of season
snippet: Chelsea's all-time top scorer in the WSL, Sam Kerr, will leave the club when her contract expires this summer.

title: Italians praise Catherine as they turn out for final day of trip
snippet: The Princess of Wales shone in front of large crowds in Reggio Emilia on the final day of her tour.

title: AI could put people off tech jobs and hurt the economy, warns Raspberry Pi boss
snippet: Eben Upton warns against claims that Artificial Intelligence will destroy vast numbers of computing roles over the coming years.

title: White-tailed eagles to be released in Exmoor despite farmer warnings
snippet: Some farmers fear the reintroduction of the UK's biggest bird of prey will threaten their livestock.

title: HMRC to use AI from British tech firm to spot fraud and tax return errors
snippet: Quantexa, a financial data platform, won the £175m contract to spot fraud and tax return errors.

title: French national shows symptoms on return from hantavirus-hit ship
snippet: Five passengers of the MV Hondius will be quarantined in Paris "until further notice", France's prime minister says.
